payload.author: claude
payload.body:
PROPOSED ARCHITECTURE (design-sweep result): CONFIRM core, AMEND two details.

CONFIRMED: Postgres for BOTH log and cache. events(tenant_id, event_id ULID, ts, kind, actor JSONB, payload JSONB, PK(tenant_id,event_id)), index (tenant_id, ts, event_id). Upload = INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING faithfully reproduces file-union + idempotency. Do NOT start with S3 (keep an S3-for-blobs escape hatch for future attachments only). Cache is derived/disposable, gated by applied_event_count.

AMENDMENT 1 (replay timing): incremental 'apply only new events' is UNSAFE in general. resolveDisplayNumbers de-collides over the entire create-set; a late event with an earlier ts re-sorts the sequence and can cascade-renumber. Since the cache PKs on the derived KEY-N (not ULID), a renumber rewrites that row id + every FK (parent_id, relations, history, comments). So: incremental fast-path ONLY for tail-appends (batch min ts >= max applied ts) with no number collision; else full replay-in-transaction.

AMENDMENT 2 (isolation): RLS keyed on a tenant_id session GUC is the enforcement floor, but TODAY THERE IS NO TENANT CONCEPT (isolation = path hash; resolveFromRequest trusts an unauthenticated header). tenant_id/claim shape is owned by SCP-122; consume it, and add app-layer scoping (fail closed) as defense-in-depth, not RLS alone.

Deps: SCP-122 (claim shape, hard blocker), SCP-125 (consumes the newly-applied + renumbered set as fan-out source), SCP-128 (split actor).

SUB-EPIC: SCP-139. CHILD TICKETS: SCP-140 (schema), SCP-141 (replay port + golden test), SCP-142 (idempotent upload primitive), SCP-143 (incremental replay w/ tail-append invariant), SCP-144 (RLS + app-layer isolation), SCP-145 (local-log migration).
